slides.title: Understanding Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
slides.subtitle: An Introduction to CNNs in Computer Vision

slides.title: What are CNNs?
slides.content: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are a class of deep learning algorithms primarily used for analyzing visual imagery. They are designed to automatically and adaptively learn spatial hierarchies of features from input images.

slides.title: CNN Architecture
slides.content: A typical CNN is composed of multiple layers, including convolutional layers, pooling layers, and fully connected layers. Each layer transforms the input data, allowing the network to learn complex representations.

slides.title: Key Components of CNNs
slides.content: Convolutional layers apply filters to input data, pooling layers reduce dimensionality, and fully connected layers output the final prediction.

slides.title: Applications of CNNs
slides.content: CNNs are widely used in image and video recognition, recommender systems, image classification, medical image analysis, and natural language processing.

slides.title: Example: Image Classification
slides.content: CNNs are highly effective in classifying images by learning patterns and features at different levels of abstraction.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: CNNs have transformed the field of computer vision with their ability to learn from data and improve over time. They continue to be a critical area of research and application in AI and machine learning.
